title: Libanotus asiaticus
intro: Libanotus asiaticus, also known as Japanese mountain pepper, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, characterized by its unique, aromatic leaves and seeds. The plant has been traditionally used in Asian medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Libanotus asiaticus include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Libanotus asiaticus is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into tinctures and extracts, which are believed to aid in promoting overall well-being.
